15.reason: [The condition checks are incorrect: it should ensure both characters are of the same type and within valid ranges. For example, `s[j-1] >= '0'` but missing `s[j-1] <= '9'`, similarly for letters. Also, when j=0, s[j-1] accesses s[-1], causing undefined behavior.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The condition checks are incorrect: it should ensure both characters are of the same type and within valid ranges. For example, `s[j-1] >= '0'` but missing `s[j-1] <= '9'`, similarly for letters. Also, when j=0, s[j-1] accesses s[-1], causing undefined behavior.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The condition checks only lower bound for left character and upper bound for right character, but does not ensure both characters are of the same type (e.g., '0' <= s[j-1] <= '9' AND '0' <= s[j+1] <= '9'). This can lead to invalid expansions like '0-z'. Also, accessing s[j-1] when j=0 or s[j+1] when j=i causes out-of-bounds access.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 1.0 , reason:  [The condition checks are incorrect: it should verify both characters are in the same category (e.g., both digits, both lowercase, or both uppercase). Current logic allows mixed cases like '0' and 'z' to pass because it only checks lower bound of left char and upper bound of right char without ensuring they belong to the same group. Also, boundary conditions may cause out-of-bounds access when j=0 or j=i (since s[j-1] or s[j+1] may be invalid).]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 1.0 , reason:  [The condition checks only lower bound for the left character and upper bound for the right character, but does not ensure both characters are of the same valid type (e.g., '0' <= s[j-1] <= '9' and '0' <= s[j+1] <= '9'). This can lead to incorrect expansions like '0-z'. Also, accessing s[j-1] when j=0 or s[j+1] when j=i causes out-of-bounds access.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 1.0 , reason:  [Incorrect boundary checks: should verify both ends are in the same category, e.g., s[j-1] >= '0' && s[j+1] >= '0', but current code only checks lower bound for left and upper bound for right, allowing invalid cross-type cases like '0'-'z']
